user_query: I’m an aspiring geology student who looks up to Bahal Tambunan, and I want to grow my credibility by sharing a concise, source-backed LinkedIn thread. Please create a mini campaign based on my interests. Inputs to use: - input/interests.json (contains the topics and my idol’s name) - input/hashtags.txt (approved hashtags list) What to produce (create directories if they don’t exist): 1) output/sources/sources.csv — A structured inventory of sources you found on the web for each topic. Use a search engine to find at least two authoritative, publicly accessible sources per topic from official organizations (e.g., national geological surveys, NASA/Earthdata, NOAA, USGS), universities, or peer-reviewed journals. Avoid wikis, generic aggregators, or commercial SEO blogs. CSV columns (exact header order): topic, source_title, organization, url. Include exactly two rows per topic from the input. 2) output/thread/thread.md — A LinkedIn-ready thread outline using the sources from the CSV. Structure: - Post 1 (Intro): A short intro that mentions Bahal Tambunan as inspiration and my goal as a student geologist; include at least two hashtags that appear in input/hashtags.txt. - One post per topic (in the same order as in input/interests.json): For each topic, include a 1–2 sentence takeaway and the exact two URLs recorded in output/sources/sources.csv for that topic; include at least two hashtags from input/hashtags.txt. - Final post (Closing): Invite feedback or additional resources; include at least two hashtags from input/hashtags.txt. Constraints and cross-checks: - Read topics and idol_name from input/interests.json and only use hashtags listed in input/hashtags.txt (case-sensitive, no extra hashtags). - sources.csv must contain exactly two rows per topic (no more, no less). - Every topic post in thread.md must reference exactly the same two URLs captured for that topic in sources.csv. - The intro must explicitly mention "Bahal Tambunan" by name. - Inspect the input directory to load both files before generating outputs, and ensure the output directories (output/sources and output/thread) exist. This should be achievable on a normal computer using web searches; do not rely on private tools or accounts. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
